creator: rea-fbsd@codelabs.ru
text:
Wesley,

Tue, Dec 23, 2008 at 12:31:12PM -0500, Wesley Shields wrote:
> The more I look at your patch the more I think you're going about it the
> wrong way.  There is a check_geom-1.3.tar.gz file available at the
> master site.  We should be using that rather than keeping a local copy
> of the script.  The attached patch addresses this fact.  Do you approve?

I implanted the script source to the port itself for the following
reason: when I first did the port, there were check_geom.tar.gz.  But it
was disappeared after 1.3 was introduced.  So I fear that 1.3 will in
turn disappear when 1.4 will be out.  That was the sole reason for doing
the things in the way I did.

The attached variant is fine with me, but given the fact that the source
itself is only 3K in size and if the distribution archive will disappear
again, then one will need to download the stuff from ftp.freebsd.org (if
the archive will be cached by the ports cluster run, otherwise the port
will be unbuildable), I think that it is better to guard ourselves from
this.
-- 
Eygene

 /  ' `         ,       __.--'      #  to read the on-line manual
 )/' _/     \   `-_,   /            #  while single-stepping the kernel.
